ENG-001_Production_Deployment_SOP: Production Deployment SOP 1. Purpose This SOP defines the mandatory process for deploying software changes to the production environment. It ensures all deployments are planned, reviewed, tested, and reversible, minimising risk to system availability and data integrity. 2. Deployment Prerequisites All code merged to main branch via approved pull request (minimum 2 reviewers) All automated tests passing in CI pipeline (unit, integration, smoke) QA sign-off on staging environment for any user-facing changes Change request ticket raised and approved in ticketing system Rollback plan documented in the change ticket Deployment window confirmed: standard window is Tue/Thu 14:00–16:00 UTC 3. Deployment Steps 4. Rollback Procedure If error rate increases >1% or P1 alert fires: initiate rollback immediately Rollback trigger authority: on-call engineer, engineering manager, or CTO Rollback via pipeline: re-run previous release tag through CI/CD Notify #incidents and #deployments channels within 5 minutes of decision Post-incident review required within 48 hours for any rollback event 5. Prohibited Actions No direct SSH access to production servers for code changes (hotfix process applies) No deployments during Friday 17:00 UTC to Monday 08:00 UTC (weekend freeze) No deployments during declared incident windows Document ID ENG-001 Version: 1.0 Department Engineering June 2026 Step Action Owner 1 Announce deployment in #deployments Slack channel Release Engineer 2 Take DB snapshot and verify backup DevOps 3 Deploy to production via CI/CD pipeline Release Engineer 4 Run smoke tests post-deploy QA / Engineer 5 Monitor error rates and latency for 30 min On-call Engineer 6 Close change ticket and post confirmation Release Engineer
ENG-002_Incident_Response_Procedure: Incident Response Procedure 1. Incident Severity Levels 2. Incident Response Lifecycle Phase 1: Detection & Alerting Alert fires via PagerDuty to on-call engineer On-call acknowledges within 5 minutes (P1) or 30 minutes (P2) Phase 2: Triage & Escalation On-call assesses severity and assigns incident owner P1/P2: Notify Engineering Manager and VP Engineering immediately Phase 3: Mitigation Isolate the problem, apply temporary fix or rollback if needed Post regular updates in #incidents channel every 15 minutes (P1) Phase 4: Resolution & Review Confirm full resolution and close incident ticket Blameless post-mortem within 48h (P1) or 5 days (P2) 3. Communication Templates Status Page Update: 'We are currently investigating an issue affecting [feature]. Our team is actively working on a resolution. Next update in 30 minutes.' Resolution Update: 'The issue affecting [feature] has been resolved as of [time]. We are monitoring the situation and will share a post-mortem within 48 hours.' 4. Post-Mortem Requirements Timeline of events from first alert to resolution Root cause analysis (5 Whys methodology) Contributing factors and detection gaps Action items with owners and due dates (SLA: completed within 2 weeks) Document ID ENG-002 Version: 1.0 Department Engineering June 2026 Severity Impact Response Time Example P1 — Critical Full outage / data loss < 15 minutes Site down, DB corruption P2 — High Major feature broken < 1 hour Login failing for all users P3 — Medium Partial degradation < 4 hours Slow API, non-critical error P4 — Low Minor / cosmetic issue Next sprint UI misalignment
ENG-003_API_Security_Guidelines: API Security Guidelines 1. Authentication & Authorisation All external APIs must use OAuth 2.0 with short-lived JWTs (max 1-hour expiry) Internal service-to-service calls must use mutual TLS (mTLS) or signed service tokens Implement Role-Based Access Control (RBAC); principle of least privilege enforced API keys must be rotated every 90 days and stored in secrets manager (e.g., AWS Secrets Manager) 2. Input Validation Validate all inputs server-side; never trust client-side validation alone Sanitise all string inputs to prevent SQL injection and XSS Enforce strict type checking and maximum payload sizes (default: 1MB) Reject requests with unexpected fields (strict schema validation) 3. Rate Limiting & Throttling 4. Logging & Monitoring Log all API requests: timestamp, IP, user ID, endpoint, response code (no PII in logs) Alert on: 5xx error rates >0.5%, 401/403 spike >10x baseline, unusual geographic access Retain API logs for minimum 90 days; security logs for 1 year 5. OWASP Top 10 Controls A01 Broken Access Control: enforce authorisation checks on every endpoint A02 Cryptographic Failures: TLS 1.2+ only; no MD5/SHA-1 for secrets A03 Injection: parameterised queries, ORM, schema validation A07 Auth Failures: account lockout after 5 failed attempts; MFA required for admin APIs A09 Logging Failures: structured logging to SIEM, tamper-proof audit trail Document ID ENG-003 Version: 1.0 Department Engineering June 2026 API Tier Rate Limit Burst Limit Response on Breach Public / Unauthenticated 60 req/min 100 req/min HTTP 429 Authenticated User 300 req/min 500 req/min HTTP 429 Internal Service 2000 req/min 5000 req/min HTTP 429 + alert
ENG-006_Production_Deployment_SOP_v2_0: Production Deployment SOP v2.0 1. Purpose & Version Notes Version 2.0 introduces mandatory canary deployments for all backend services, AI-assisted deployment readiness checks, and an updated rollback SLA. Supersedes ENG-001 v1.0. Effective immediately for all engineering teams. 2. Deployment Strategy (Updated) 3. AI Deployment Readiness Check (New) Before each deployment, run deploy-check CLI — AI scans diff for high-risk patterns Checks: DB migration without index, N+1 query patterns, removal of auth middleware HIGH risk: deployment blocked until Engineering Manager approval MEDIUM risk: proceeds with mandatory 1-hour post-deploy monitoring LOW / CLEAR: standard deployment process applies 4. Updated Deployment Steps 5. Rollback SLA (Updated) P1 trigger: rollback decision within 5 min, execution completed within 10 min (was 15 min in v1.0) Automated rollback trigger: error rate >2% for 3 consecutive minutes (was >1% in v1.0) Canary rollback: reduce canary to 0% — no full rollback needed if still in canary phase 6. Extended Freeze Window (Updated) Weekend freeze: Friday 14:00 UTC to Monday 10:00 UTC (was Monday 08:00 UTC in ENG-001) Additional freeze: 72 hours before and after any major product release Emergency hotfix exception: CTO approval required + dedicated on-call resource confirmed 7. Change Log Document ID ENG-006 Version: 2.0 Department Engineering June 2026 Service Type Strategy Traffic Split Observation Period Backend / API Canary 5% → 25% → 100% 15 min per step Frontend / UI Blue-Green 0% → 100% 30 min monitoring Database migrations Rolling + feature flag N/A 1 hour post-deploy Hotfix / P1 patch Direct with rollback plan N/A 30 min hypercare Step Action Owner 1 Run deploy-check; resolve HIGH flags Release Engineer 2 Announce in #deployments with risk level Release Engineer 3 DB snapshot verified DevOps 4 Deploy canary (5%) via CI/CD Release Engineer 5 Monitor error rate + latency 15 min On-call Engineer 6 Promote to 25%, then 100% with checks Release Engineer 7 Close ticket; post confirmation Release Engineer Version Doc ID Date Changes 1.0 ENG-001 Jan 2025 Initial release 2.0 ENG-006 Jun 2026 Canary deploy, AI readiness check, updated rollback SLA
ENG-007_Code_Review_Guidelines: Code Review Guidelines 1. Purpose These guidelines establish consistent standards for conducting and participating in code reviews across all engineering teams. Effective review improves code quality, knowledge sharing, and collective ownership while maintaining velocity. 2. When a Review is Required All code merged to main/master/develop must be reviewed — no exceptions Minimum 2 approvals: database schema changes, auth/security changes, public API changes Minimum 1 approval: all other changes including hotfixes (async review permitted post-merge) Self-merges prohibited unless designated solo maintainer on isolated repo 3. Pull Request Standards PR size: aim for under 400 lines changed; large PRs must be split unless architecturally necessary PR title format: [TYPE] Short description — e.g. [feat] Add OAuth2 login flow PR description must include: What changed, Why it changed, How to test, Screenshots if UI Link the relevant ticket/issue in every PR without exception 4. Reviewer Responsibilities Respond within 1 business day (P1/hotfix: within 2 hours) Label comments: Blocking (must fix), Suggestion (optional), Question (understanding only) Focus on: correctness, security, performance, readability, test coverage — not style (use linter) Be specific and constructive — explain the problem and suggest a fix Approve only when all blocking comments are fully resolved 5. Review SLAs Document ID ENG-007 Version: 1.0 Department Engineering June 2026 Priority First Review SLA Merge SLA P1 Hotfix 2 hours Same day High Priority Feature 4 hours 1 business day Standard 1 business day 3 business days Chore / Docs / Refactor 2 business days 1 week
ENG-008_Disaster_Recovery_Plan: Disaster Recovery Plan 1. Purpose & Scope This Disaster Recovery Plan (DRP) defines procedures for restoring business-critical systems following a disaster event including data centre failures, cloud provider outages, ransomware attacks, and natural disasters. 2. Recovery Objectives 3. Disaster Scenarios & Response 3.1 Cloud Region Outage Automated failover to secondary region triggers within 5 minutes via Route53 health checks On-call engineer confirms failover; monitors 30 minutes before declaring recovery 3.2 Database Corruption Restore from last verified snapshot (point-in-time recovery enabled on all Tier 1 DBs) Data integrity validation scripts run before re-enabling write traffic 3.3 Ransomware / Cyber Attack Immediate: isolate affected systems; engage incident response retainer Do NOT pay ransom without Legal and CEO approval Restore from air-gapped offline backup; validate integrity before use 4. DR Team & Responsibilities 5. Testing Schedule Tabletop exercise: quarterly Partial failover test: bi-annually (Tier 1 regional failover in low-traffic window) Full DR simulation: annually — measure actual RTO/RPO against targets Test results documented; gaps remediated within 30 days Document ID ENG-008 Version: 1.0 Department Engineering June 2026 System Tier RTO RPO Examples Tier 1 — Critical 1 hour 15 minutes Core product, payments, auth Tier 2 — Important 4 hours 1 hour Admin dashboard, reporting, APIs Tier 3 — Standard 24 hours 4 hours Internal tools, documentation Tier 4 — Non-critical 72 hours 24 hours Marketing sites, archives Role Responsibility Backup DR Coordinator (CTO) Declare disaster, lead response VP Engineering Infrastructure Lead Execute failover, restore systems Senior DevOps Engineer Security Lead Contain breach, forensics External IR Retainer Communications Lead Internal + external comms COO Legal Lead Regulatory notifications External Counsel
FIN-001_Expense_Reimbursement_Policy: Expense Reimbursement Policy 1. Purpose This policy establishes the rules, limits, and procedures for reimbursing employees for legitimate business expenses incurred in the course of their duties. It ensures fair treatment, fiscal responsibility, and compliance with tax regulations. 2. Eligible Expense Categories 3. Submission Process Submit expenses via the Expense Management System within 30 days of incurrence Attach original receipts (digital acceptable if clearly legible) Claims exceeding INR 10,000 / GBP 300 require line manager approval before submission All foreign currency claims must include the exchange rate on the date of expense 4. Approval Workflow 5. Reimbursement Timeline Approved claims are processed in the next payroll cycle Emergency advances may be requested via Finance for urgent travel Rejected claims will be notified within 5 working days with reasons 6. Non-Reimbursable Items Personal purchases, alcohol beyond a hospitality context, fines or penalties Expenses not supported by valid receipts Expenses incurred without prior approval where required Document ID FIN-001 Version: 1.0 Department Finance June 2026 Category Per-Day Limit (INR) Per-Day Limit (GBP) Receipt Required Meals & Entertainment INR 1,500 GBP 40 Yes (>INR 500 / GBP 15) Local Travel (cab/auto) INR 800 GBP 25 Yes Domestic Air Travel Actual (Economy) Actual (Economy) Yes + approval International Travel As per travel policy As per travel policy Yes + pre-approval Accommodation INR 6,000/night GBP 150/night Yes Communication INR 500/month GBP 20/month Yes Claim Amount Approver Up to INR 5,000 / GBP 150 Line Manager INR 5,001–25,000 / GBP 151–750 Department Head Above INR 25,000 / GBP 750 Finance Director
FIN-002_Payroll_Processing_SOP: Payroll Processing SOP 1. Purpose This SOP defines the end-to-end payroll processing cycle, roles, timelines, and controls to ensure all employees are paid accurately and on time, in compliance with applicable statutory requirements. 2. Payroll Calendar 3. Statutory Deductions (India) Provident Fund (PF): 12% employee + 12% employer on basic salary Employee State Insurance (ESIC): 0.75% employee + 3.25% employer Professional Tax: as per state slab TDS on Salary: as per Income Tax Act, Sec 192, based on declared investments 4. Controls & Reconciliation Four-eye check: payroll prepared by one analyst, reviewed by Payroll Manager Monthly reconciliation of payroll register vs. bank transfer advice Variance report generated for any month-on-month change exceeding 5% Annual Form 16 issued to all employees by 31st May 5. Off-Cycle Payments Off-cycle payments (joining bonuses, severance, corrections) require written approval from the CFO and must be processed within 3 working days of approval. Finance logs all off-cycle transactions separately. Document ID FIN-002 Version: 1.0 Department Finance June 2026 Activity Deadline Owner HR inputs (joiners, leavers, changes) 15th of month HR Team Payroll data lock 17th of month Payroll Manager Payroll computation & review 19th–20th of month Finance Management approval 21st of month CFO / Finance Director Bank file submission 22nd of month Finance Salary credit date Last working day Bank Payslip distribution 1st of following month HR / Finance
FIN-003_Quarterly_Budget_Report: Quarterly Budget Report 1. Overview This template defines the structure and process for preparing and presenting the Quarterly Budget Report to senior leadership. It covers actuals vs. budget variance, department-level commentary, and forward-looking projections. 2. Report Structure 3. Variance Thresholds Green: variance within +/- 5% of budget — no action required Amber: variance of 5%–15% — department head commentary required Red: variance exceeding 15% — CFO review and corrective action plan required 4. Preparation Timeline 5. Distribution Board of Directors: full report (PDF) via secure board portal Department Heads: department-specific sections only All-staff summary: high-level narrative (internal intranet) Document ID FIN-003 Version: 1.0 Department Finance June 2026 Section Content Format Executive Summary Key highlights, variances, risks 1 page narrative P&L vs Budget Revenue, COGS, OpEx comparison Table + chart Department Spend Cost centre breakdown Table Headcount & HR Costs FTE count, salary, benefits Table Cash Flow Summary Operating, investing, financing Table Q+1 Forecast Next quarter projection Table + commentary Task Due Date Owner Close monthly books 5th of month following quarter end Finance Department cost submissions 7th of month Dept. Heads Finance consolidation 10th of month FP&A CFO review 12th of month CFO Board presentation 15th of month CFO / CEO
FIN-005_Expense_Reimbursement_Policy_v2_: Expense Reimbursement Policy v2.0 1. Purpose & Version Notes Version 2.0 raises expense limits to reflect 2026 inflation adjustments, mandates AI-powered receipt scanning for all submissions, and introduces a Sustainability Travel Incentive. Supersedes FIN-001 v1.0. Effective 1st July 2026. 2. Updated Expense Limits (2026) 3. AI Receipt Scanning (New in v2.0) All receipts must be submitted via the Expense App — manual PDF uploads no longer accepted AI scanner auto-extracts vendor, amount, date, and category — employee confirms or corrects Duplicate receipt detection: system flags receipts matching amount + vendor + date within 30 days Paper receipts: photograph within 24 hours; originals not required for amounts under INR 2,000 4. Sustainability Travel Incentive (New) Train travel reimbursed at 120% of ticket cost when chosen over an equivalent domestic flight Electric vehicle rental: INR 500 bonus credited per rental vs equivalent petrol vehicle Carbon offset contribution of INR 200 auto-added to all approved domestic flight claims 5. Approval Workflow (Unchanged from FIN-001) 6. Change Log Document ID FIN-005 Version: 2.0 Department Finance June 2026 Category v1.0 Limit (INR) v2.0 Limit (INR) Change Meals & Entertainment 1,500/day 2,000/day +33% Local Travel 800/day 1,200/day +50% Accommodation (domestic) 6,000/night 7,500/night +25% Communication 500/month 750/month +50% Ergonomic Equipment 12,000/year 18,000/year +50% Claim Amount Approver Up to INR 5,000 Line Manager INR 5,001–25,000 Department Head Above INR 25,000 Finance Director Version Doc ID Date Changes 1.0 FIN-001 Jan 2025 Initial release 2.0 FIN-005 Jul 2026 Limit revisions, AI scanning, sustainability incentive
FIN-006_US_Payroll_Processing_SOP: US Payroll Processing SOP 1. Purpose This SOP defines payroll processing for US-based employees, in compliance with the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA), IRS requirements, and applicable state wage and hour laws. It is the US region variant of FIN-002. 2. US Payroll Calendar 3. Federal & State Deductions Federal Income Tax: withheld per IRS Publication 15 based on W-4 FICA — Social Security: 6.2% employee + 6.2% employer (wage base USD 168,600 for 2026) FICA — Medicare: 1.45% employee + 1.45% employer; additional 0.9% over USD 200,000 FUTA: 6.0% employer-only on first USD 7,000 per employee (effective 0.6% after state credit) 401(k) pre-tax deferral: up to USD 23,500 (2026 IRS limit); employer match per plan document 4. Non-Exempt Overtime Rules FLSA: overtime at 1.5x regular rate for all hours over 40 in a workweek California: daily overtime at 1.5x for hours 8–12; 2x for hours beyond 12 in a single day Unapproved overtime still must be paid — manager approval does not override FLSA obligation 5. Year-End Tax Filings Document ID FIN-006 Version: 1.0 Department Finance June 2026 Activity Deadline Owner HR inputs (new hires, terminations, changes) 3rd of pay period HR Timesheet approval (non-exempt employees) 5th of pay period Line Managers Payroll data lock 7th of pay period Payroll Manager Payroll computation & review 8th–9th Finance IRS Form 941 quarterly deposit check 15th of following month Finance Direct deposit issued Bi-weekly Friday Finance / Bank Pay stub distribution Same day as payment Payroll System Form Recipient Deadline W-2 All employees January 31 W-3 Social Security Administration January 31 1099-NEC Independent contractors (>USD 600) January 31 940 (FUTA) IRS January 31 941 (Q4) IRS January 31
FIN-007_Travel_and_Entertainment_Policy: Travel and Entertainment Policy 1. Purpose This policy governs all business travel and entertainment expenditure to ensure cost-effectiveness, employee safety, and compliance with applicable tax regulations. Applies to all employees travelling or entertaining on behalf of the company. 2. Pre-Travel Approval Domestic travel: line manager approval minimum 5 business days in advance International travel: Department Head + Finance approval minimum 10 business days in advance High-risk destinations (FCDO/State Department): additional Security + CEO approval required Book through company travel portal (Preferred Vendor: XYZ Travel) to access negotiated rates 3. Air Travel Standards 4. Hotel Standards 5. Entertainment Guidelines Client entertainment requires: attendees, company, and business purpose documented Alcohol in client meals: permitted up to 20% of total bill; spirits-only claims not reimbursable Entertainment for government officials: pre-approval from Legal required (anti-bribery) Team events: up to INR 3,000 / GBP 75 / USD 100 per head with manager approval Gifts to clients: max INR 2,500 / GBP 50 / USD 75; cash gifts strictly prohibited 6. Submission & Compliance All T&E claims submitted within 30 days of trip end via Expense App Late submissions (30–60 days): accepted with manager justification Claims over 60 days: not reimbursed except in extenuating circumstances Finance conducts quarterly T&E audit; non-compliance may result in disciplinary action Document ID FIN-007 Version: 1.0 Department Finance June 2026 Route Duration Class Permitted Upgrade Conditions Under 4 hours Economy only No upgrade permitted 4–8 hours Economy (Premium on approval) Medical approval required Over 8 hours Business class Standard for Director+ Any (VP+) Business class No additional approval needed Location Max/Night (INR) Max/Night (GBP) Max/Night (USD) Tier 1 Cities (Mumbai, Delhi, London, NYC) 10,000 200 250 Tier 2 Cities 6,000 120 150 International (other) 8,000 160 200
HR-001_India_Employee_Onboarding_SOP: India Employee Onboarding SOP 1. Purpose This Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) defines the structured onboarding process for all new employees joining the organization at any India-based office. It ensures a consistent, compliant, and welcoming experience for every new hire. 2. Scope Applicable to all full-time, part-time, and contract employees hired across India offices including Bangalore, Mumbai, Hyderabad, and Pune. 3. Pre-Joining Checklist Send offer letter and employment agreement for e-signature (minimum 5 working days before joining) Collect KYC documents: Aadhaar, PAN card, passport (if applicable) Verify educational and employment background (third-party BGV vendor) Set up payroll account with designated bank partner Prepare laptop, access cards, and workspace Send pre-joining welcome email with first-day instructions 4. Day 1 Activities 4.1 HR Induction Introduction to company history, mission, and values Walkthrough of HR policies: leave, attendance, code of conduct Enrolment in ESIC / PF as per statutory requirements 4.2 IT Setup Issue system and corporate email ID Configure VPN, Slack, and collaboration tools Mandatory security training completion 5. 30-60-90 Day Plan 6. Compliance & Statutory Obligations PF registration under Employees' Provident Funds Act, 1952 ESIC enrollment for eligible employees (salary up to INR 21,000/month) Professional Tax deduction as per state regulations Form 11 and Form 2 submission within 30 days of joining Document ID HR-001 Version: 1.0 Department Human Resources June 2026 Milestone Activities Owner Day 1–30 Role orientation, buddy assignment, tool access HR + Manager Day 31–60 Project ramp-up, performance goal setting Manager Day 61–90 First review, feedback collection, confirmation HR + Manager
HR-002_UK_Employee_Onboarding_SOP: UK Employee Onboarding SOP 1. Purpose This SOP outlines the onboarding framework for all new employees joining UK offices, ensuring legal compliance with UK employment law and providing a professional, consistent induction experience. 2. Legal Requirements Right to Work check must be completed before or on Day 1 (Immigration, Asylum and Nationality Act 2006) Issue written statement of employment particulars within Day 1 (Employment Rights Act 1996) Enrol eligible employees in workplace pension (The Pensions Act 2008 — Auto-Enrolment) Register for PAYE with HMRC and issue P45/starter checklist 3. Pre-Joining Process Issue employment contract for e-signature (10 working days before start) Collect P45 or complete new starter checklist Conduct Right to Work document verification (certified copy retained) DBS check (if role requires it) Send welcome pack with company handbook and IT onboarding guide 4. Induction Programme 5. Benefits Enrolment Workplace pension auto-enrolment (3% employer / 5% employee minimum) Private medical insurance election window (30 days from start) Cycle to Work and Season Ticket Loan schemes 28 days statutory annual leave (inclusive of bank holidays) explained Document ID HR-002 Version: 1.0 Department Human Resources June 2026 Day Activity Responsible Day 1 Welcome briefing, IT setup, office tour HR Day 2–3 Team introductions, role-specific training Line Manager Week 2 Compliance training, data protection (UK GDPR) HR + Legal Month 1 Probation goals set, buddy programme begins Line Manager
HR-003_Remote_Work_Policy: Remote Work Policy 1. Purpose & Scope This policy governs remote and hybrid working arrangements for all employees globally. It sets expectations for availability, data security, equipment, and performance management for employees working outside a primary company office. 2. Eligibility Employees must have completed 90-day probation period Role must be deemed suitable for remote working (approved by line manager + HR) No active performance improvement plan (PIP) in place 3. Work Arrangement Types 4. Employee Responsibilities Maintain a dedicated, ergonomic, and distraction-minimised workspace Be available and responsive during agreed core hours (10:00–16:00 local time) Use company-approved VPN for all work activity Attend all mandatory video calls with camera on (unless exception approved) Report any home-workspace injury or incident to HR within 24 hours 5. Equipment & Expenses Company provides: laptop, headset, and security token Home internet subsidy: up to GBP 30 / INR 2,500 per month (on receipt) Employees may claim ergonomic equipment up to GBP 150 / INR 12,000 annually All company equipment must be returned within 5 business days of departure 6. Data Security Requirements No use of public Wi-Fi without VPN active Lock screen when away from device (auto-lock at 5 minutes) No printing of confidential documents at home Report lost or stolen devices to IT Security immediately Document ID HR-003 Version: 1.0 Department Human Resources June 2026 Type Office Days / Week Description Full Remote 0 Fully home-based; quarterly office visit required Hybrid — Core 2–3 Agreed fixed days in office Hybrid — Flex 1–2 Flexible days, team-coordinated Office-First 4–5 Primarily office-based with occasional WFH
HR-004_India_Employee_Onboarding_SOP_v2_: India Employee Onboarding SOP v2.0 1. Purpose & Version Notes Version 2.0 updates this SOP to reflect the Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023 (DPDPA), a revised BGV vendor process, and new hybrid-work onboarding tracks introduced in Q1 2026. Supersedes HR-001 v1.0. All HR teams must transition by 1st August 2026. 2. Scope Applicable to all full-time, part-time, contract, and gig employees hired across India offices and remote India-based hires, including new Tier-2 city hiring under the 2026 Expansion Programme. 3. Pre-Joining Checklist (Updated) Send digitally signed offer letter via DigiLocker-integrated HRMS (minimum 7 working days before joining — increased from 5) Collect KYC: Aadhaar, PAN, DigiLocker-verified degree certificates DPDPA-compliant consent form collected for all personal data processing BGV initiated via updated vendor (XYZ Verify Pvt. Ltd.) — turnaround 5 working days Bank account setup via HDFC / ICICI salary account API integration Remote hires: courier laptop + peripherals (address verified with Aadhaar) 4. Onboarding Tracks (New in v2.0) 5. 30-60-90 Day Plan (Revised) 6. DPDPA Compliance (New in v2.0) Explicit consent obtained before processing Aadhaar, health, or biometric data Employee given access to company Data Principal Rights portal on Day 1 Data retention limited to employment period + 7 years post-departure HR must complete DPDPA awareness training by 30th July 2026 7. Change Log Document ID HR-004 Version: 2.0 Department Human Resources June 2026 Track Employee Type Duration Mode Office Track HQ/Branch-based 5 days In-person Remote Track WFH/Tier-2 city 5 days Virtual (Zoom + LMS) Executive Track Director and above 10 days Hybrid + C-suite meetings Milestone Activities New in v2.0 Owner Day 1–30 Orientation, tool access, buddy DPDPA training module HR + Manager Day 31–60 Project ramp-up, OKR setting AI tool onboarding session Manager Day 61–90 Performance review, confirmation 360 feedback pilot HR + Manager Version Doc ID Date Changes 1.0 HR-001 Jan 2025 Initial release 2.0 HR-004 Jun 2026 DPDPA compliance, remote track, BGV update
HR-005_UK_Employee_Onboarding_SOP_v2_0: UK Employee Onboarding SOP v2.0 1. Purpose & Version Notes Version 2.0 incorporates updated Right to Work digital check requirements (effective January 2026), revised pension contribution rates, and a new AI-assisted onboarding portal rollout. Supersedes HR-002 v1.0. 2. Right to Work — Updated Process Digital RTW checks now mandatory for all new hires via certified Identity Service Provider (IDSP) Physical document checks retained only where IDSP is unavailable (e.g. non-UK passport holders) Retain digital RTW record for duration of employment + 2 years Retrospective checks NOT required for employees onboarded under HR-002 v1.0 3. Pension — Revised Contributions (2026) 4. AI Onboarding Portal (New in v2.0) New hires complete digital induction via Onboarding Hub (AI-powered Q&A for policy questions) Portal auto-generates personalised Day 1 checklist based on role, department, and location IT provisioning requests auto-triggered upon contract signature Line manager receives daily onboarding progress summary for first 30 days 5. Revised Induction Schedule 6. Change Log Document ID HR-005 Version: 2.0 Department Human Resources June 2026 Scheme Employee % Employer % Effective Date Auto-Enrolment (Minimum) 5% 3% April 2024 (unchanged) Enhanced Scheme (Opt-in) 6% 6% January 2026 (new) Executive Scheme (Director+) 7% 10% January 2026 (new) Day Activity New in v2.0 Owner Day 1 RTW check, AI portal setup, welcome Digital RTW via IDSP HR Day 2–3 Team intro, role training AI portal self-serve modules Line Manager Week 2 UK GDPR + AI usage policy training AI ethics module added Legal + HR Month 1 Probation goals, buddy programme OKR tool onboarding Line Manager Version Doc ID Date Changes 1.0 HR-002 Jan 2025 Initial release 2.0 HR-005 Jun 2026 Digital RTW, pension update, AI portal
HR-006_US_Remote_Work_Policy: US Remote Work Policy 1. Purpose & Scope This policy governs remote and hybrid working for all US-based employees. It is a region-specific variant of the global Remote Work Policy (HR-003), adapted for US employment law, state tax nexus rules, and ADA requirements. 2. State Tax Nexus Warning Employees wishing to work remotely from a state other than their designated work state must obtain prior written approval from HR and Finance before doing so. Unapproved cross-state remote work is prohibited. Approval required minimum 30 days before intended start date Finance assesses state payroll tax, income tax withholding, and nexus implications Temporary remote work under 14 days per year in the same state may be exempt — confirm with Finance 3. Work Arrangement Types 4. Equipment & Home Office Stipend Company provides: laptop, headset, and security token (shipped to home address) Home office stipend: USD 75/month for internet and utilities (taxable benefit) One-time ergonomic equipment allowance: up to USD 500 per employee California employees: company must reimburse all necessary business expenses (CA Labor Code Section 2802) 5. ADA & Accommodation Remote work may be approved as a reasonable accommodation — route through HR only, not line manager Medical documentation required; reviewed confidentially by HR Accommodation decisions documented and reviewed annually 6. Data Security VPN required for all access to internal systems CCPA: no personal data of California residents to be processed on personal or unsecured devices Screen lock required; auto-lock at 5 minutes of inactivity Document ID HR-006 Version: 1.0 Department Human Resources June 2026 Type Office Days / Week FLSA Note Full Remote 0 Non-exempt: hours tracked; overtime applies Hybrid — Core 2–3 Non-exempt: schedule documented in writing Hybrid — Flex 1–2 Exempt: performance-based, no hour tracking Office-First 4–5 Exempt or non-exempt per role classification
HR-007_Performance_Review_Policy: Performance Review Policy 1. Purpose This policy establishes a fair, consistent, and transparent performance review process for all employees. It defines review cycles, rating frameworks, calibration procedures, and the link between performance and compensation. 2. Review Cycles 3. Rating Scale 4. Calibration Process Calibration sessions held by HR for each department after manager ratings submitted Facilitator: HR Business Partner; attendees: all people managers in department Distribution targets are guidelines, not hard quotas — justification required for deviations Calibrated ratings finalised by HR + Department Head; no changes by individual managers post-calibration 5. Link to Compensation 6. Employee Rights Employee receives written copy of final rating and review summary Employee may submit a written rebuttal within 10 working days of receiving rating Rebuttal reviewed by HR Director; outcome communicated within 15 working days Document ID HR-007 Version: 1.0 Department Human Resources June 2026 Review Type Frequency Participants Output Probation Review End of 90-day probation Employee + Manager Confirm / extend / exit Mid-Year Check-in July Employee + Manager Progress vs goals; no rating Annual Review December Employee + Manager + HR Rating + comp recommendation 360 Feedback Annual (senior roles) Peers + direct reports Developmental input only Rating Label Description Expected % 5 Exceptional Consistently exceeds all goals; role model ~5% 4 Exceeds Expectations Exceeds most goals; high impact ~20% 3 Meets Expectations Achieves all core goals; solid performer ~55% 2 Partially Meets Achieves some goals; improvement needed ~15% 1 Below Expectations Does not meet goals; PIP required ~5% Rating Merit Increase Range Bonus Eligibility 5 — Exceptional 8–12% 150% of target bonus 4 — Exceeds 5–8% 120% of target bonus 3 — Meets 2–5% 100% of target bonus 2 — Partially Meets 0–2% 50% of target bonus 1 — Below 0% No bonus
LEG-001_Vendor_NDA: Vendor NDA Parties This Non-Disclosure Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of the Effective Date between [Company Name], a company incorporated under the laws of [Jurisdiction] ("Disclosing Party"), and the vendor identified below ("Receiving Party"). 1. Definition of Confidential Information "Confidential Information" means any data or information, oral or written, disclosed by the Disclosing Party that is designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential given the nature of the information and circumstances of disclosure, including but not limited to: business plans, financial data, technology, customer data, product roadmaps, and trade secrets. 2. Obligations of Receiving Party Hold Confidential Information in strict confidence using at least the same degree of care used to protect its own confidential information (no less than reasonable care) Use Confidential Information solely for the purpose of evaluating or executing the business relationship Not disclose Confidential Information to any third party without prior written consent Limit disclosure to employees or subcontractors with a need to know who are bound by equivalent confidentiality obligations 3. Exclusions Obligations under this Agreement do not apply to information that: (a) is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the Receiving Party; (b) was known to the Receiving Party prior to disclosure; (c) is independently developed without use of Confidential Information; or (d) is required to be disclosed by law or court order (with prior written notice to Disclosing Party). 4. Term This Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of [3 years] from the Effective Date. Confidentiality obligations for trade secrets shall survive indefinitely. 5. Governing Law This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of [Jurisdiction]. Any disputes shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of [Jurisdiction]. Signatures Document ID LEG-001 Version: 1.0 Department Legal June 2026 Disclosing Party Receiving Party Name: _______________________ Name: _______________________ Title: ________________________ Title: ________________________ Date: ________________________ Date: ________________________ Signature: ___________________ Signature: ___________________
LEG-002_Data_Privacy_Policy: Data Privacy Policy 1. Introduction This Data Privacy Policy explains how [Company Name] collects, processes, stores, and protects personal data in accordance with applicable data protection laws, including the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), the UK GDPR, and India's Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023 (DPDPA). 2. Data We Collect 3. Individual Rights Right to Access: request a copy of personal data held (response within 30 days) Right to Rectification: correct inaccurate or incomplete data Right to Erasure ('Right to be Forgotten'): where no legal obligation to retain Right to Restrict Processing: during disputes or objection review Right to Data Portability: receive data in structured, machine-readable format Right to Object: to processing based on legitimate interests 4. Data Retention 5. Data Breach Notification GDPR/UK GDPR: notify supervisory authority within 72 hours of awareness of a breach likely to risk individual rights DPDPA (India): notify Data Protection Board as prescribed; notify affected individuals without undue delay Internal escalation: DPO must be notified within 24 hours of suspected breach Document ID LEG-002 Version: 1.0 Department Legal June 2026 Data Category Examples Legal Basis Identity Data Name, employee ID, passport Contract, Legal Obligation Contact Data Email, phone, address Contract, Legitimate Interest Financial Data Bank account, salary details Contract, Legal Obligation Usage Data System access logs, IP address Legitimate Interest Sensitive Data Health, biometrics (if applicable) Explicit Consent Data Type Retention Period Basis Employee records 7 years post-departure Legal obligation Payroll records 8 years Tax law CCTV footage 30 days Security policy System access logs 1 year Security/audit
LEG-003_Service_Agreement_Template: Service Agreement Template Parties & Recitals This Service Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of [Effective Date] between [Client Name] ("Client") and [Service Provider Name] ("Service Provider"). The parties agree that Service Provider will deliver the services described herein on the terms set out in this Agreement. 1. Scope of Services Service Provider agrees to deliver the following services as detailed in Schedule A attached hereto: [Description of Services]. Any changes to scope must be agreed in writing via a signed Change Order. 2. Fees & Payment Terms 3. Term & Termination Initial term: [12 months] from Effective Date Either party may terminate for convenience with [30 days] written notice Either party may terminate immediately for material breach unremedied within [14 days] of notice On termination: all work product and data returned within 10 business days 4. Intellectual Property All work product, deliverables, and materials created by Service Provider under this Agreement shall be owned by [Client / Service Provider — select one] upon full payment of fees. Pre-existing IP of each party remains the sole property of that party. 5. Liability & Indemnification Service Provider liability is limited to the total fees paid in the preceding 3 months Neither party liable for indirect, consequential, or punitive damages Each party to indemnify the other against third-party claims arising from their own negligence or breach Document ID LEG-003 Version: 1.0 Department Legal June 2026 Item Amount Due Date Retainer / Monthly Fee [Amount] 1st of each month Project Milestone 1 [Amount] [Date] Project Milestone 2 [Amount] [Date] Expenses (reimbursable) Actuals + [0]% Monthly with invoice
LEG-005_US_Vendor_NDA: US Vendor NDA Parties This Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement is entered into as of the Effective Date between [Company Name], a Delaware corporation ("Company"), and the vendor identified below ("Vendor"). This is the US region variant of the global Vendor NDA (LEG-001). 1. Definition of Confidential Information "Confidential Information" means any non-public information disclosed by either party that is designated as confidential or that a reasonable person would understand to be confidential, including trade secrets as defined under the Defend Trade Secrets Act (DTSA), 18 U.S.C. § 1836 et seq. 2. DTSA Immunity Notice Pursuant to 18 U.S.C. § 1833(b), an individual shall not be held criminally or civilly liable under any federal or state trade secret law for the disclosure of a trade secret made in confidence to a government official or attorney solely for the purpose of reporting a suspected violation of law. 3. Obligations Each party holds the other's Confidential Information in strict confidence Use Confidential Information solely for evaluating or executing the business relationship No disclosure to third parties without prior written consent Employees and contractors with access must be bound by equivalent written confidentiality obligations 4. Exclusions Obligations do not apply to information that: (a) is in the public domain without breach; (b) was rightfully known prior to disclosure; (c) is independently developed; or (d) is required by law, court order, or SEC/regulatory disclosure — with prompt prior written notice where legally permissible. 5. Term & Governing Law Term: 3 years from Effective Date; trade secret obligations survive indefinitely Governing Law: State of Delaware, without regard to conflict of law principles Venue: state and federal courts of Delaware; exclusive jurisdiction consented to Injunctive relief: either party may seek equitable relief without posting bond 6. Signatures Document ID LEG-005 Version: 1.0 Department Legal June 2026 Company Vendor Name: ___________________ Name: ___________________ Title: ____________________ Title: ____________________ Date: ____________________ Date: ____________________ Signature: _______________ Signature: _______________
LEG-006_Employee_IP_Assignment_Agreement: Employee IP Assignment Agreement Parties This Intellectual Property Assignment Agreement is entered into between [Company Name] ("Company") and the employee named below ("Employee") as a condition of employment, supplementing the Employee's employment contract. 1. Assignment of Work Product Employee irrevocably assigns to the Company all right, title, and interest in all Inventions created, conceived, or developed during the period of employment that: (a) relate to the Company's business or R&D; (b) result from work performed for the Company; or (c) are developed using Company resources, time, or information. 2. Definition of Inventions Software, code, algorithms, and related documentation Patents, patent applications, and patentable ideas Copyrightable works including written materials, designs, and creative content Trade secrets, know-how, and proprietary processes Trademarks, brand assets, and domain names developed in scope of work 3. Excluded Inventions Inventions developed entirely on Employee's own time, without Company resources, and that do not relate to Company's business are excluded. Employee must disclose all potential exclusions in Schedule A at time of signing. 4. Employee Obligations Promptly disclose all Inventions to the Company in writing Execute any documents needed to perfect the Company's ownership (Company bears costs) Obligations survive termination for 12 months for Inventions conceived during employment 5. Signatures Document ID LEG-006 Version: 1.0 Department Legal June 2026 Company Employee Name: ___________________ Name: ___________________ Title: ____________________ Title: ____________________ Date: ____________________ Date: ____________________ Signature: _______________ Signature: _______________
OPS-001_Client_Onboarding_Process: Client Onboarding Process 1. Purpose This document defines the end-to-end process for onboarding new clients, from contract signature to go-live. A smooth onboarding experience is critical to client retention, satisfaction, and long-term revenue. 2. Onboarding Phases 3. Kickoff Meeting Agenda Introductions and team overview (15 min) Review of contract scope and success criteria (20 min) Project timeline and milestone walkthrough (15 min) Communication cadence and escalation path (10 min) Q&A and next steps (15 min) 4. Client Health Scoring 5. Handoff Criteria (Implementation to CSM) All integrations live and tested Admin users trained and signed off Handoff call completed with documented notes Client satisfaction score (CSAT) collected at go-live Document ID OPS-001 Version: 1.0 Department Operations June 2026 Phase Activities Duration Owner Kickoff Intro call, stakeholder mapping, success criteria Day 1 Account Manager Discovery Requirements gathering, integration review Week 1–2 Solutions Engineer Setup Account config, data migration, user provisioning Week 2–4 Implementation Training Admin and end-user training sessions Week 4–5 Customer Success Go-Live Launch, hypercare period begins Week 5–6 Account Manager Steady State QBRs, health scoring, expansion Ongoing CSM Score Status Action 8–10 Healthy Standard QBR cadence 5–7 At Risk Monthly check-in, action plan 1–4 Critical Exec escalation, recovery plan
OPS-002_Escalation_Matrix: Escalation Matrix 1. Purpose This Escalation Matrix defines the escalation path for unresolved issues across client-facing and internal operations. Clear escalation paths reduce resolution time and protect client relationships. 2. Client Issue Escalation 3. Internal Escalation Paths 3.1 Technical Issues L1: Support Engineer → L2: Senior Engineer → L3: Engineering Manager → L4: CTO 3.2 Finance / Billing Issues L1: Billing Analyst → L2: Finance Manager → L3: CFO 3.3 Legal / Compliance Issues L1: Operations Analyst → L2: General Counsel → L3: CEO 3.4 HR Issues L1: HR Business Partner → L2: HR Director → L3: CEO 4. Escalation Contacts 5. Escalation Rules Escalations must be documented in the ticketing system with full context Escalating party owns communication to the client until handover is confirmed Post-resolution: root cause documented within 5 business days Document ID OPS-002 Version: 1.0 Department Operations June 2026 Level Trigger Escalate To Timeframe L1 Issue not resolved in 24h Senior Support Engineer 24 hours L2 Issue not resolved in 48h or client distressed Customer Success Manager 48 hours L3 Issue not resolved in 72h or NPS risk VP Customer Success 72 hours L4 Churn risk or SLA breach COO / CEO Immediate Role Name Email Phone VP Customer Success [Name] [email] [phone] Engineering Manager [Name] [email] [phone] CFO [Name] [email] [phone] General Counsel [Name] [email] [phone] COO [Name] [email] [phone] CEO [Name] [email] [phone]
OPS-003_Internal_Audit_Checklist: Internal Audit Checklist 1. Purpose This checklist is used by Operations and Finance to conduct quarterly internal audits across key business functions. It ensures controls are operating effectively and identifies risks before external audits. 2. Financial Controls 3. HR & Compliance 4. IT & Security 5. Operations 6. Audit Sign-Off Document ID OPS-003 Version: 1.0 Department Operations June 2026 Control Check Status Notes Bank reconciliation Completed monthly and signed off [ ] Expense approval workflow All claims approved per policy [ ] Vendor invoice matching 3-way match: PO, receipt, invoice [ ] Payroll reconciliation Agrees to HR headcount [ ] Budget vs actual review Variance report produced [ ] Control Check Status Notes Employee files All required documents on file [ ] Background checks Completed for all new hires [ ] Mandatory training All staff completed compliance training [ ] Statutory filings PF/ESIC/TDS filed and paid on time [ ] Control Check Status Notes Access reviews Quarterly user access review completed [ ] Off-boarding Leavers' access revoked within 24h [ ] Backup verification Recovery test performed this quarter [ ] Vulnerability scanning Scan completed; critical issues remediated [ ] Control Check Status Notes Vendor contracts All active contracts current and signed [ ] SLA compliance Customer SLAs met this quarter [ ] Incident log All incidents logged and closed [ ] Policy reviews Policies reviewed annually [ ] Role Name Date Signature Internal Auditor Operations Manager CFO
OPS-006_Client_Onboarding_Process_v2_0: Client Onboarding Process v2.0 1. Purpose & Version Notes Version 2.0 introduces a redesigned 6-phase onboarding journey, AI-assisted implementation checklist, and a revised health scoring model. Average time-to-value target reduced from 6 weeks to 4 weeks. Supersedes OPS-001 v1.0. 2. Updated Onboarding Phases 3. AI Implementation Checklist (New) AI auto-generates client-specific checklist from Discovery call transcript Each item assigned to Client or Internal with due date Weekly progress report auto-emailed to client stakeholders every Monday Blocked items flagged to Account Manager + CSM within 24 hours of becoming overdue 4. Revised Health Scoring Model 5. Time-to-Value SLA (New) SMB clients: go-live within 3 weeks — breach triggers CSM escalation Mid-market clients: go-live within 5 weeks — breach triggers VP review Enterprise clients: go-live within 10 weeks — milestone-based SLA tracked per contract 6. Change Log Document ID OPS-006 Version: 2.0 Department Operations June 2026 Phase Activities Target Duration Owner 1. Alignment Kickoff, success criteria, exec sponsor Day 1–2 Account Manager 2. Discovery Requirements, integrations, data audit Week 1 Solutions Engineer 3. Build Config, integrations, data migration Week 1–2 Implementation 4. Validate UAT, edge-case testing, sign-off Week 3 CSM + Client 5. Launch Go-live, 2-week hypercare Week 4 Account Manager 6. Optimise QBRs, expansion, health scoring Ongoing CSM Dimension Weight Key Signals Product Adoption 30% DAU, feature usage, login frequency Relationship 25% NPS, responsiveness, exec engagement Outcomes 25% Success criteria achievement rate Support Health 20% Open tickets, CSAT, escalation count Version Doc ID Date Changes 1.0 OPS-001 Jan 2025 Initial release 2.0 OPS-006 Jun 2026 6-phase model, AI checklist, health scoring, TTV SLA
OPS-007_Vendor_Management_Policy: Vendor Management Policy 1. Purpose This policy defines the framework for selecting, onboarding, managing, and offboarding third-party vendors to ensure due diligence, cost control, risk management, and compliance across all vendor relationships. 2. Vendor Classification 3. Vendor Onboarding Requirements Tier 1 & 2: complete vendor due diligence form (financial stability, security posture, compliance certs) Data-sharing vendors: Data Processing Agreement (DPA) signed before any data shared All vendors: signed NDA (LEG-001/LEG-005), service agreement (LEG-003), bank details verified Tier 1: security questionnaire with ISO 27001 / SOC 2 evidence requested Minimum 3 competing quotes required for any engagement over INR 5L / GBP 5,000 4. Vendor Performance Monitoring 5. Vendor Offboarding Provide notice per contract terms (typically 30–90 days) Data return or certified deletion confirmed in writing within 30 days of contract end All access credentials revoked on contract end date Final invoice reconciliation and vendor rating recorded in vendor register Document ID OPS-007 Version: 1.0 Department Operations June 2026 Tier Criteria Examples Review Frequency Tier 1 — Critical Core ops, data access, >INR 50L annual spend Cloud, payroll, HRMS Quarterly Tier 2 — Important Significant support, INR 10L–50L spend CRM, legal tools, marketing Bi-annually Tier 3 — Standard Non-critical, <INR 10L spend Stationery, minor SaaS Annually Metric Measurement Minimum Threshold SLA compliance Monthly SLA report 95% uptime / delivery Invoice accuracy Finance reconciliation 0 unexplained variances Response time Support ticket logs Per contract SLA Security incidents Vendor disclosure + scan 0 critical incidents/year
OPS-008_Business_Continuity_Plan: Business Continuity Plan 1. Purpose & Scope This Business Continuity Plan (BCP) ensures the organisation can continue delivering critical functions during and after a disruption, covering natural disasters, pandemics, civil unrest, supply chain disruption, and major cyber incidents. 2. Critical Business Functions 3. BCP Activation BCP activated by COO, CEO, or delegated Crisis Manager when disruption threatens 2+ critical functions Crisis Management Team (CMT) convened within 1 hour of activation All-staff notification via SMS broadcast + email within 30 minutes Customer-facing status page updated within 15 minutes of activation 4. Crisis Management Team 5. Disruption Scenarios 5.1 Office Inaccessible All staff activate remote work per HR-003 / HR-006 within 2 hours VPN capacity verified quarterly; fallback: mobile hotspot allowance auto-approved 5.2 Pandemic / Mass Illness Minimum staffing rosters activated; cross-training matrix covers absent roles Critical function coverage maintained with 30% workforce reduction tolerance 5.3 Key Person Dependency All critical roles have documented backup procedures and a named alternate Succession plan reviewed annually for Director level and above 6. BCP Testing Annual tabletop exercise: CMT walkthrough of 2 scenarios Bi-annual communication test: test SMS broadcast + escalation tree BCP reviewed and updated annually by Operations + HR + Finance Document ID OPS-008 Version: 1.0 Department Operations June 2026 Function Dept. Max Tolerable Downtime Min. Resource Level Customer support (P1/P2) Ops 2 hours 2 agents minimum Payroll processing Finance 24 hours 1 payroll analyst + manager Core product availability Engineering 1 hour 1 on-call engineer Financial reporting Finance 48 hours 1 finance analyst Legal / regulatory response Legal 4 hours General Counsel access Role Primary Alternate Crisis Manager COO CFO Communications Lead Head of Marketing HR Director IT / Systems Lead CTO VP Engineering HR Lead HR Director HR Business Partner Finance Lead CFO Finance Manager
